\title{3\textdegree{} Longitudinal Periodicity in the UNESCO World Heritage Corpus:
Enrichment in Domed Monuments and Harmonic Cluster Asymmetry}

\author[1]{Seth Tenenbaum}[https://orcid.org/0009-0008-5797-2498]

\authormark{TENENBAUM}
\titlemark{3\textdegree{} LONGITUDE PERIODICITY IN THE UNESCO WORLD HERITAGE CORPUS}

\address[1]{\orgname{Independent Scholar,}
\orgaddress{\country{USA}}}

\corres{Seth Tenenbaum (\email{seth@fourthtemple.com})}

\fundingInfo{This research received no external funding.}

\keywords{
longitude periodicity | spatial statistics | Levant | UNESCO World Heritage |
3-degree harmonics | domed architecture | archaeoastronomy |
monument distribution | Mount Gerizim
}

\abstract[ABSTRACT]{
Domed and spherical UNESCO Cultural and Mixed World Heritage monuments
($N=\NcircTotal$ of $N=\NclusterTotal$ cultural sites) are significantly
enriched within $\pm$0.15\textdegree{} of a pre-specified 3\textdegree{}
longitudinal grid anchored to Mount Gerizim (35.269\textdegree{}E).
Three enrichment tests survive Bonferroni correction at $k=\BonfK$:
full corpus ($p_{\mathrm{adj}}=\pAdjTestOne$), domed monuments
($p_{\mathrm{adj}}=\pAdjTestTwo$), and harmonic cluster asymmetry
($p_{\mathrm{adj}}=\pAdjTestThree$); all are cross-checked by permutation
null models. The period and anchor are stated hypotheses from historical and
metrological sources; unit-sweep sensitivity analysis confirms 3\textdegree{}
as the dominant spacing \emph{given} the pre-specified anchor. The Wikidata
Q180987 stupa corpus and OWTRAD route network independently reproduce the
pattern. The pipeline is fully reproducible.
}
